title: Peppery Juicy Norwegian Potato Biryani
description: A hearty, peppery twist on classic biryani using Norwegian potatoes and dairyfree ingredients, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.

ingredients.name: Potatoes
ingredients.notes: peeled and diced into 1/2 inch cubes

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced potatoes, biryani masala, and a pinch of salt; toss to coat the potatoes evenly.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cover. Cook the potatoes until just tender, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, rinse the basmati rice until the water runs clear. In a separate pot, bring 400 ml of water to a boil, add the rice, cover, and simmer for 12 minutes until cooked. Drain any excess water.
When the potatoes are tender, fluff them with a fork and stir in the cooked rice, mixing thoroughly.
Add the lemon juice and chopped cilantro, stirring to combine. Adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
Transfer the biryani to a serving dish and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra cilantro or a squeeze of lemon.
Enjoy a comforting, peppery Norwegian take on biryani!
